title: Mega Pinsir Battle Guide
summary: A [Bug]/[Flying] physical cleaner that turns [return] into a boosted [Flying] STAB via [aerilate], with [quick-attack] as a priority finisher.
strengths: 155 Attack and 105 Speed outrun every base form in the tier
weaknesses: [stealth-rock] removes 25 percent of HP on every entry
Turn one is still the base ability before mega evolving

recommendedMoves.reason: Main boosted [Flying] STAB after [aerilate] kicks in
One setup turn makes Mega Pinsir a game-ending breaker

recommendedItems.reason: Required mega stone for the Pinsir mega form

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Pinsir is a [Bug]/[Flying] physical [sweeper] defined by [aerilate] — [Normal] moves like [return] become 1.2x boosted [Flying] STAB, and that single line of text turns a mediocre base form into one of the cleanest cleaners in the tier. 155 Attack paired with 105 Speed outpaces most neutral threats, and with a [swords-dance] up it tears through almost every wall in the format within two hits. Manage one pre-mega turn and it usually ends the game on the spot.

## Strengths
The [aerilate] combination of [return] and [quick-attack] is the whole pitch. [return] produces one of the hardest-hitting single-target attacks in the tier once the item is active, and [quick-attack] gets the same [conversion] — a [Flying]-type priority move with full STAB and ability boost. Coverage choices like [close-combat], [earthquake], and [stone-edge] all exist, so any [Steel], [Rock], [Electric], or [Fairy] wall can be punished with the correct pick.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The defining problem is [stealth-rock]. A [Flying] typing under rocks loses 25 percent on every switch, so a [rapid-spin] or [defog] partner is effectively mandatory. [Electric], [Ice], and [Rock] all hit for 2x, and the weak special defense means any priority or fast special attacker becomes a [revenge] threat. Turn one is still the base ability, so the first switch-in must bait a move it can survive pre-evolution.

## Recommended Moveset
[return] is the locked main attack because [aerilate] gives it both STAB and the 1.2 boost. [close-combat] is mandatory coverage, since [Rock] and [Steel] pivots like rotom-wash or tyranitar otherwise wall the [Flying] STAB outright. [quick-attack] fills the cleaner slot — [aerilate] priority is what punishes choice-scarf users that [revenge] below it. The fourth slot is [swords-dance], because one clean setup turn against a wall pretty much closes the game. [earthquake] or [stone-edge] can replace [close-combat] if you prefer broader coverage.

## Item · Ability · Tera
The item is always [pinsirite] — there is no alternate build. The ability locks to [aerilate] after mega evolving. Tera [Flying] maxes [return] damage and turns Mega Pinsir into a pure [flying] cleaner, while Tera [Ground] is the defensive option that eats [Electric] and upgrades the [earthquake] slot if you run it.

## Teammates & Synergy
Hazard removal is effectively required — a dedicated [rapid-spin] or [defog] user in every team slot. Fast pivots like [u-turn] users create the free swap-in turns that Mega Pinsir needs to set up, and an Intimidate holder on the back line mitigates priority-based revenges. A [Ground] partner that answers [Electric] attackers closes the last defensive hole before Mega Pinsir comes in.

## Play Patterns
The ideal sequence is: clear rocks, bring Mega Pinsir in on a forced switch, mega evolve, [swords-dance] on the read, and start chaining [return] KOs. Save [quick-attack] for the very last turn — it is priority so it clears weakened [revenge] threats you can't outspeed normally. Tera is held for the moment an [Ice], [Electric], or [Rock] move is telegraphed.
